diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Throbbing headache is one limb of the classic paroxysmal tetrad of catecholamine excess.
Sudden severe headache must trigger consideration of intracranial haemorrhage during a hypertensive crisis.
Drenching sweats with pallor during paroxysms is a catecholamine-surge signature and argues against pure panic.
Profound diaphoresis with pallor and objective severe hypertension exceeds the physiology of a pure psychiatric panic event.
Beta-adrenergic drive from catecholamine excess produces palpitations and resting tachycardia.
Tachycardia and tremor overlap with thyrotoxicosis; the episodic pattern favours catecholamine excess.
Paroxysmal severe hypertension is the fourth limb of the tetrad; patient-obtainable home reading, unverified, admissible in telehealth as a self-reported value.
A reading of this magnitude with symptoms defines a hypertensive emergency demanding immediate escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent self-resolving paroxysmal attacks over two months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent paroxysmal course with spontaneous resolution is highly characteristic of an episodically-secreting catecholamine tumour.
Chronic catecholamine excess raises the metabolic rate and causes weight loss.
Catecholamine-induced volume contraction produces orthostatic hypotension between surges — a clue to the paradoxical BP swings.

symptoms.symptom_name: No decongestant/stimulant use; on no regular medications

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pseudoephedrine, cocaine or amphetamine use argues against an exogenous sympathomimetic cause of the surge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured hypertension and sinus tachycardia (BP 195/110, HR 125)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective severe hypertension with tachycardia and bounding pulses — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers a hypertensive emergency from the self-reported home reading and symptoms, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine resting tremor with brisk reflexes, no focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenergic tremor and hyper-reflexia with an intact focal neuro exam — sealed clinician examination reassuring against an established focal stroke.
Markedly elevated metanephrines are the biochemical confirmation of catecholamine excess — a hospital/laboratory test never available in real time via telehealth.
Cross-sectional adrenal imaging localises the tumour after biochemical confirmation — imaging is impossible via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Episodic throbbing headache + profuse diaphoresis + palpitations + paroxysmal hypertension together are highly specific for a catecholamine-secreting tumour and define this as a hypertensive emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer for in-hospital assessment (BP control, ECG, troponin)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A hypertensive emergency with possible ACS/stroke needs immediate hospital assessment and controlled blood-pressure reduction the AI cannot provide.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plasma free or 24-hour urinary fractionated metanephrines
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line biochemical confirmation of catecholamine excess — arranged by the hospital/endocrine team, not remotely.
Localises the tumour once metanephrines confirm; specialist-directed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home blood-pressure self-monitoring as the sole management
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Repeated home readings are not a substitute for emergency escalation in an active hypertensive emergency and must not delay 000.
